name: npc-mcp
description: Embeds an AI presence inside a Minecraft server via a Fabric mod companion. Exposes a layered perception stack and curated command tools so an MCP client can act as an unseen participant in the game world. The paired Fabric mod dials out to the host: it POSTs world events to npc_ingest_event and polls npc_drain_commands for queued actions, so the cartridge itself holds no socket.

auth.notes: Perception reads are unauthenticated. Command tools (npc_say, npc_give, npc_execute_command) are gated by the loaded persona; with no persona loaded they fail closed.

tools.name: npc_get_narrative_context
tools.description: Get the current narrative context: who is online, what they are doing, the atmosphere of the world. Read-only.

tools.name: npc_get_world_state
tools.description: Get a structured snapshot of the current Minecraft world state (players, server vitals). Read-only.

tools.name: npc_get_recent_events
tools.description: Return the most recent raw JSONL events the mod has reported, newest last. Read-only.

count.description: How many recent events to return (default 20)

tools.name: npc_ingest_event
tools.description: Feed one protocol v1 event envelope into the perception stack. Called by the Fabric mod as it dials out to the host. The arguments object is the event envelope itself.

tools.name: npc_drain_commands
tools.description: Return and clear the queue of protocol v1 command envelopes awaiting delivery, newline-delimited. The Fabric mod polls this and executes each command in-game.

tools.name: npc_say
tools.description: Speak as the ghost in Minecraft chat. Persona-gated; queued for the mod to execute.

tools.name: npc_give
tools.description: Give an item to a player. Persona-gated; queued for the mod to execute.

tools.name: npc_execute_command
tools.description: Run an arbitrary Minecraft server command. Persona-gated; queued for the mod to execute.

tools.name: npc_load_persona
tools.description: Load a persona configuration. The arguments object is the persona definition itself (name, allowed tools, voice). Until a persona is loaded, command tools fail closed.
